created_by: Wilderculture WILD Toolbox session (Claude) — woodland group batch; field sheet 10 split per type. v3.3.

ukhab_level5_states.w1b5: Lime-maple woodlands of rocky slopes (Annex I H9180)

ukhab_identity.ukhab_level5_note: Keyed at w1b. w1b5 (H9180 Tilio-Acerion) is the Annex I type; w1b6 other upland mixed ashwoods.
ukhab_identity.annex1_habitat: H9180 Tilio-Acerion forests of slopes/screes (the w1b5 type)

sources.title: UK Habitat Classification v2.01 (official definitions + master data sheet + applied surveys)
sources.specific_claim: w1b = Upland mixed ashwoods (w1b5 lime-maple H9180, w1b6 other). Confirmed in official UKHab definitions and applied surveys (e.g. SSEN Kintore 'w1b Upland mixed ashwoods').

nvc_communities: W9 Fraxinus excelsior–Sorbus aucuparia–Mercurialis perennis woodland (upland)
cross_references.nvc_communities_notes: W9 is the upland mixed ashwood; the woodland of base-rich and limestone ground, with the richest woodland ground flora in the uplands.
cross_references.bng_metric_habitat_name: Woodland and forest — Upland mixed ashwoods

sources.title: NVC Field Guide to Woodland (W9) / UK BAP Upland Mixed Ashwoods
sources.specific_claim: W9 is the upland mixed ashwood community on base-rich ground.

ecology.description: Woodland of base-rich and limestone ground — ash with wych elm, hazel, field maple over the richest woodland ground flora in the uplands. The wooded climax on the limestone country that the upland calcareous grassland (g2b) succeeds towards. Now being reshaped by ash dieback. As with all the woodland, the management question is browsing/regeneration, not stocking.
condition_indicators_good: Full age range incl. regeneration and deadwood
The rich base-rich ground flora — dog's mercury, ramsons, wood anemone, herb-paris
Native species dominant; glades and graded edge
Browsing light enough that regeneration survives
condition_indicators_poor: Overgrazed: no regeneration, ground flora stripped
Ash-dieback gaps left unmanaged or over-felled
Sycamore invasion; deadwood tidied away; even-aged with no continuity
ecosystem_functions: The richest woodland ground flora in the uplands
Soil-builder and carbon store on base-rich ground
High energy flow and deadwood/saproxylic habitat
Mast and browse (a regeneration constraint, not a grazing asset)
ecology.whole_system_role: The wooded climax of the base-rich/limestone uplands — the destination the calcareous grassland leads to if released, and a high-diversity flagship.

successional_neighbours.note: Calcareous grassland → tall herb & scrub (hawthorn, juniper, ash) → upland mixed ashwood (the limestone climax).

sources.title: Wilderculture field sheet 10 — Native Woodland
sources.specific_claim: Ashwood ground flora, ash dieback, regeneration diagnostic.

ukhab_condition.condition_grade_note: Assigned per parcel; regeneration is the diagnostic.

condition_grade_descriptors.label: Favourable
condition_grade_descriptors.description: Full age range with regeneration; rich base-rich ground flora; native dominant; deadwood retained.

condition_grade_descriptors.label: Unfavourable recovering
condition_grade_descriptors.description: Browsing reduced; regeneration and dieback-resistant ash retained; sycamore controlled.

condition_grade_descriptors.label: Unfavourable no change
condition_grade_descriptors.description: Mature canopy, sparse regeneration; flora reduced.

condition_grade_descriptors.label: Unfavourable declining
condition_grade_descriptors.description: Overgrazed (no regeneration) or dieback-gapped and over-felled; sycamore invading.

condition_grade_descriptors.label: Destroyed / converted
condition_grade_descriptors.description: Canopy lost with no continuity, or replaced.

sources.title: Woodland Condition Assessment
sources.specific_claim: Regeneration and tree-health (incl. disease) are core condition indicators.

abiotic_template.altitude_range_m: Valley floor to the natural treeline
abiotic_template.aspect_exposure: Base-rich slopes and screes; rocky ground for the H9180 type
abiotic_template.bioclimate_notes: Yorkshire Dales and limestone country above all.

elemental_conditions.convention_note: CURRENT-STATE reading. Protect & Restore (rich, but reshaped by dieback).

water.descriptor: Thin to moderate soils over base-rich rock.

earth.descriptor: Calcium-rich, biologically active, freely cycling — the richest woodland soils.

fire.descriptor: High light capture and energy flow; rich ground flora and deadwood community.

range_of_natural_variability: New ash/mixed woodland on released calcareous grassland
successional_envelope.successional_pathway: calcareous grassland → tall herb & scrub → upland mixed ashwood (the limestone climax). NOT to displace species-rich calcareous grassland (g2b) where that open habitat is the priority.

wild_assessment.energy_flow_role: High energy flow with the richest ground flora — a base-rich woodland flagship, now in flux from ash dieback.
wild_assessment.wild_zone_rationale: Protect & Restore — high-diversity, base-rich, often ancient. New ashwood/mixed woodland regenerating on released calcareous ground is Regenerate→Bioregionally Wilder. But do NOT let woodland displace species-rich calcareous grassland (g2b) where the open habitat is the priority — the classic 'right tree, right place' tension here.
wild_assessment.disturbance_regime: Browsing-controlled. Reduce deer/sheep so regeneration survives; retain potentially dieback-resistant ash; control sycamore.

wild_management_target.detail: Sustain natural regeneration by reducing browsing; manage ash dieback FOR natural resistance (retain potentially resistant trees, fell only for genuine safety, keep the deadwood); control sycamore; keep deadwood and veterans. Balance with the calcareous grassland it succeeds from.

wild_assessment.ecological_value_notes: High — the richest woodland ground flora in the uplands and an Annex I type (H9180), though currently reshaped and uncertain because of ash dieback.

rarity_irreplaceability.notes: Base-rich/limestone woodland is geology-restricted; ancient stands irreplaceable; dieback adds uncertainty.

diversity.notes: The richest woodland ground flora in the uplands.

energy_flow_contribution.notes: High woodland energy flow and deadwood habitat.

naturalistic_lu_ha.value: Near nil — reduce browsing; any conservation grazing low and site-specific
naturalistic_lu_ha.basis_note: As for all native woodland: reduce deer/sheep to allow regeneration; light conservation grazing only where needed for structure.

wild_assessment.mosaic_role_note: The base-rich wooded climax; browse/mast a regeneration constraint, not a grazing patch.
wild_assessment.preferential_grazing_magnet_note: Browse is high-value — the reason regeneration fails under heavy grazing.
wild_assessment.process_indicators_note: Ash dieback makes resistance-led management central here.

forage.overall_palatability_notes: Tree browse and the rich ground flora are high-value — which is exactly why regeneration fails under heavy grazing. Not a managed forage resource.

forage_yield_by_grade.notes: Not a forage/stocking habitat — managed by browsing level for regeneration.

forage.utilisation_factor_note: Not applicable — browsing-managed, not DM × utilisation.

conservation_lu_ha.value: Near nil — reduce browsing to allow regeneration; light, site-specific conservation grazing only where needed
conservation_lu_ha.scheme: site-specific; not a standard stocking figure
conservation_lu_ha.basis_note: Reduce deer/sheep first; the base-rich ground flora is vulnerable to overgrazing.

sources.title: Woodland Trust grazing/regeneration guidance
sources.specific_claim: Browsing reduction is the primary action for native woodland regeneration.

forage.palatability_rank: High (browse + ground flora) — a regeneration liability
forage.nutritional_quality_note: Soil-builder; no toxicity theme. Browse nutritious — keep browsing low for regeneration.

optimal_grazing_animals.notes: Reduced browsing is the goal; light conservation grazing only where structure needs it.

unsuitable_animals.reason: Prevent regeneration and strip the rich ground flora.

timing_windows.preferred_season: Reduce year-round browsing; any conservation grazing low and site-specific
timing_windows.avoid: Heavy browsing; premature felling of dieback ash
timing_windows.per_species_notes: Deer management primary in Scotland; sheep elsewhere.

management.intensity_guidance: Reduce browsing first. Manage ash dieback FOR resistance — retain possibly resistant trees, fell only for genuine safety, keep deadwood.
management.regulatory_note: Ash dieback (Hymenoscyphus fraxineus) expected to kill 50–75% of ash — do NOT fell prematurely; retain potentially resistant trees, manage for safety only, keep the deadwood. Control sycamore. Biosecurity matters. Live, moving disease area — verify current Forest Research / GOV.UK guidance; check designated-site consents.
management.monitoring_protocol: Regeneration, ground flora, ash-dieback severity and resistant-tree retention, sycamore ingress, deadwood. Apply the re-grade trigger.

bng_unit_value.value_note: Very high distinctiveness — priority habitat (H9180). High BNG value; ancient stands irreplaceable.

sources.title: Statutory Biodiversity Metric
sources.specific_claim: Upland mixed ashwood very high distinctiveness; confirm exact label.

key_positive_indicators: rich base-rich ground flora — Mercurialis perennis (dog's mercury), Allium ursinum (ramsons), Anemone nemorosa, Paris quadrifolia (herb-paris)
integrity_indicators_fauna: saproxylic deadwood beetles and flies; the rich snail fauna of base-rich woods
integrity_indicators_flora: the rich base-rich ground flora is itself the headline integrity indicator — among the richest woodland floras in the uplands
species_list.note: Skeleton only — full records in Phase 2B. Ash-dieback resistance status is a key tree-level attribute to track.

fields_fully_populated: ukhab_identity (w1b verified — the code the Crosswalk omitted)
the g2b→w1b succession and 'right tree right place' tension
data_completeness.notes: The base-rich woodland the Crosswalk missed (it listed w1a/w1d/w1e but not w1b). Richest woodland ground flora in the uplands; Protect & Restore; reshaped by ash dieback (manage for resistance, not pre-emptive felling). The wooded climax of the calcareous-grassland succession — but not to displace species-rich g2b where that open habitat is the priority.

sources.title: UK Habitat Classification v2.01
sources.specific_claim: w1b upland mixed ashwoods; w1b5 H9180.

sources.title: Forest Research / GOV.UK — ash dieback (Hymenoscyphus fraxineus)
sources.specific_claim: 50–75% ash mortality predicted; manage for natural resistance, retain resistant trees.
